sources.name: GDELT
sources.notes: Use for topic detection, clustering, geography hints, and media-attention bursts. Do not use as the sole basis for a final bet.
sources.license_notes: Free/open platform; verify reuse terms per downstream workflow.

sources.name: World Bank Indicators API
sources.notes: Use for structural baselines and country comparisons.
sources.license_notes: Open World Bank data; confirm per-series usage terms if redistributed.

sources.name: ILOSTAT Bulk Download Facility
sources.notes: Use the official bulk indicator CSV downloads for labour structure, participation, unemployment, sector dynamics, and informality.
sources.license_notes: Public bulk download access exposed by ILOSTAT; verify per-dataset reuse terms if redistributed.

sources.name: USGS Earthquake Feeds
sources.notes: Use for precise hazard confirmation with timestamps and magnitude.
sources.license_notes: Machine-readable feeds exposed publicly by USGS.

sources.name: OpenStreetMap Overpass API
sources.update_cadence: Near real time with some lag
sources.notes: Use for infrastructure density, place context, proximity checks, and asset lookup.
sources.license_notes: Read-only API over OSM data; respect OSM/instance usage expectations.

sources.name: EIA API
sources.coverage: Energy markets, electricity, fuels, and system data
sources.notes: Use for energy system context and supply-demand stress.
sources.license_notes: Free API, but requires an API key.

raw_signals.signal_title: Urban exposure remains high in Japan
raw_signals.signal_summary: A highly urbanized population means seismic risk and infrastructure resilience have large operational consequences. Latest observed value for Japan: 92.1901718034531.
Recent earthquake activity provides an official reminder that seismic risk remains live rather than theoretical. Strongest matching event magnitude: 4.8.

raw_signals.signal_title: Urban population share continues to rise
raw_signals.signal_summary: Urban growth increases demand pressure on already stressed water and cooling systems. Latest observed value for India: 35.3788972368389.

raw_signals.signal_title: Youth unemployment remains a useful stress signal
raw_signals.signal_summary: Youth and early-career employment data help show whether first-rung labour market access is tightening. Latest observed value for United States: 9.34.

dimensions.indicator: Unemployment, youth total (% of total labor force ages 15-24) (modeled ILO estimate)

entities: Unemployment, youth total (% of total labor force ages 15-24) (modeled ILO estimate)

signal_convergences.convergence_title: Seismic retrofit and resilience systems in Japan
signal_convergences.convergence_summary: Preparedness coverage, dense urban exposure, and recent official seismic activity together make resilience systems more strategically important.

paired_signals.why_it_matters: Official hazard activity strengthens the case for preparedness as a practical systems priority.
paired_signals.connection: When current seismic activity or preparedness attention meets high urban exposure, resilience systems become more strategically important.

paired_signals.signal_title: Urban exposure remains high in Japan
paired_signals.why_it_matters: Dense urban systems increase the value of preparedness, retrofit, and rapid-response infrastructure.
paired_signals.connection: When current seismic activity or preparedness attention meets high urban exposure, resilience systems become more strategically important.

signal_convergences.inference: Preparedness coverage, dense urban exposure, and recent official seismic activity together make resilience systems more strategically important.
signal_convergences.reasoning_notes: Convergence score 1.23 using 1 recent/active event signals plus structural context.

future_bets.title: Seismic retrofit and resilience systems in Japan
future_bets.summary: Preparedness coverage, dense urban exposure, and recent official seismic activity together make resilience systems more strategically important.
future_bets.implication: Builders, insurers, urban operators, and infrastructure teams should pay attention to retrofit, monitoring, and response systems rather than treating preparedness as solved.
future_bets.why_now: Seismic preparedness matters more when official event data, live coverage, and urban exposure all point to continuing relevance.

paired_signals.why_it_matters: Official hazard activity strengthens the case for preparedness as a practical systems priority.
paired_signals.connection: When current seismic activity or preparedness attention meets high urban exposure, resilience systems become more strategically important.

paired_signals.signal_title: Urban exposure remains high in Japan
paired_signals.why_it_matters: Dense urban systems increase the value of preparedness, retrofit, and rapid-response infrastructure.
paired_signals.connection: When current seismic activity or preparedness attention meets high urban exposure, resilience systems become more strategically important.

second_order_effects: Building diagnostics, emergency logistics, and infrastructure monitoring tools become more relevant.
Insurance, compliance, and public procurement may reward better resilience evidence.
what_to_watch: Recurring official seismic events near major populated areas.
Policy, retrofit, or preparedness investment linked to urban infrastructure.
what_could_change_this: A long quiet period could reduce urgency in practice.
If resilience systems are already saturated in the market, growth may be narrower than it appears.
